definition: Build an inverted index from nonzero training features, accumulate candidate scores only for train items sharing informative features with an evaluation item, and retain the exact or approximate top-k candidates.

scale_profile.best_for: Sparse high-dimensional features where most train-eval dot products are zero or obviously small.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions of sparse vectors when postings lists remain selective.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Features are dense, uninformative, or every item shares most features.

tunable_parameters.name: posting_pruning
tunable_parameters.purpose: Drops very common or low-weight features that create large candidate sets.

tunable_parameters.name: score_upper_bound
tunable_parameters.purpose: Prunes candidates that cannot enter the top-k.

quality_gates: Compare top-k recall with exact sparse multiplication on a subset.
Report feature normalization and pruning rules.
Check whether rare-feature artifacts dominate the nearest-neighbor graph.
failure_modes: Common features can create near-all-pairs candidate explosion.
Aggressive pruning can remove biologically or scientifically meaningful weak overlap.
Sparse MIPS scores are sensitive to vector norm choices.
example_usage: Use dense_vector_topk_similarity.py for small sparse matrices exported as columns; for production replace with an inverted-index scorer that emits the same CSV columns.

citations.title: Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search for Sparse Data in Less Space
citations.evidence: Studies approximate top-k maximum inner product search for sparse vectors and memory/latency/accuracy tradeoffs.

citations.title: Seismic: Efficient and Scalable Sparse Vector Search with Inverted Indexing
citations.evidence: Describes scalable sparse-vector nearest-neighbor search using inverted indexing.
